title: Topology Declarations, Policies, and Migration · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs topology declarations, policies, and migration from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a versioned Go topology ownership manifest after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Make QueueDeclare and ExchangeDeclare calls equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and classify precondition errors.
Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Topology Owner Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Topology Owner Contract RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Topology Owner Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Topology Owner Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Topology Owner Contract, keep Topology Owner Contract connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A review Go messaging team handles case 1103 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
